{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f4c8d0d73baf47febfb335dc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\t * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n5\t * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n6\t * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n7\t * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n8\t * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n9\t * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n10\t * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t\n76\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n77\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n78\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n79\t\n80\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n81\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n82\t\n83\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n84\t\n85\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n86\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n87\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n88\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n89\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n90\t\n91\t    let html = `\n92\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n93\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n94\t            <div>\n95\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n96\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n97\t            </div>\n98\t        </div>\n99\t    `;\n100\t\n101\t    // Add latest result if available\n102\t    if (latestResult) {\n103\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n104\t        html += `\n105\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n106\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n107\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n108\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n109\t                </div>\n110\t        `;\n111\t\n112\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n113\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n114\t        }\n115\t\n116\t        html += `</div>`;\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Add staleness indicator\n120\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n121\t    html += `\n122\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n123\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n124\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n125\t        </div>\n126\t    `;\n127\t\n128\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n129\t    return card;\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n133\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n134\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n135\t\n136\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n137\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n138\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n139\t    return div.innerHTML;\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t/**\n143\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n144\t *\n145\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n146\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n147\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n148\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n149\t */\n150\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n151\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n152\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n153\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n154\t        if (c == null) return;\n155\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n156\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n157\t        } else {\n158\t            node.appendChild(c);\n159\t        }\n160\t    });\n161\t    return node;\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n165\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n166\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n167\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n168\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n169\t    const rem = s % 60;\n170\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n171\t}\n172\t\n173\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n174\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n175\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n176\t\n177\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n178\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n179\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n180\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n181\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n182\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n183\t];\n184\t\n185\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n186\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n187\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n188\t// projects' supported intents\").\n189\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n190\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n191\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n192\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n193\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n194\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n195\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n196\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n197\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n198\t};\n199\t\n200\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n201\t    const out = [];\n202\t    const seen = {};\n203\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n204\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n205\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n206\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n207\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n208\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n209\t        return ad - bd;\n210\t    });\n211\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n212\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n213\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n214\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n215\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n216\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n217\t            seen[intent] = true;\n218\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n219\t        });\n220\t    });\n221\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n222\t}\n223\t\n224\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n225\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n226\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n227\t\n228\t/**\n229\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n230\t *\n231\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n232\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n233\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n234\t *\n235\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n236\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n237\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n238\t */\n239\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n240\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n241\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n242\t\n243\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n244\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n245\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n246\t    ]));\n247\t\n248\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n249\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n250\t    ]));\n251\t\n252\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n253\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n254\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n255\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n256\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n257\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n258\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n259\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n260\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n261\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n262\t        list.appendChild(li);\n263\t    });\n264\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n265\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n266\t    }\n267\t    card.appendChild(list);\n268\t\n269\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n270\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n271\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n272\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n273\t    });\n274\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n275\t\n276\t    return card;\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n280\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n281\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n282\t\n283\t/**\n284\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n285\t *\n286\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n287\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n288\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n289\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n290\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n291\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n292\t *\n293\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n294\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n295\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n296\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n297\t *\n298\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n299\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n300\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n301\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n302\t */\n303\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n304\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n305\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n306\t    }\n307\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n311\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n312\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n313\t\n314\t/**\n315\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n316\t *\n317\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n318\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n319\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n320\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n321\t *\n322\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n323\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n324\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n325\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n326\t */\n327\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n328\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n329\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n330\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n331\t    return card;\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t/**\n335\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n336\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n337\t *\n338\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n339\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n340\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n341\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n342\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n343\t */\n344\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n345\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n346\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n347\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n348\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n349\t    return card;\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t/**\n353\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n354\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n355\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n356\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n357\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n358\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n359\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n360\t *\n361\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n362\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n363\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n364\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n365\t *\n366\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n367\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n368\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n369\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n370\t */\n371\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n372\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n373\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n374\t    });\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n378\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n379\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n380\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n381\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n382\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n383\t\n384\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n385\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n386\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n387\t    ]));\n388\t\n389\t    if (utterance) {\n390\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n394\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n395\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n396\t\n397\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n398\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n399\t    ]);\n400\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n401\t\n402\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n403\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n404\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n405\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n406\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n407\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n408\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n409\t\n410\t    return card;\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n414\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n415\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n416\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n417\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n418\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n419\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n420\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n421\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t/**\n425\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n426\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n427\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n428\t */\n429\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n430\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n431\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n432\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n433\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n434\t    return elapsedMs;\n435\t}\n436\t\n437\t/**\n438\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n439\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n440\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n441\t */\n442\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n443\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n444\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n445\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n446\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n447\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n448\t    return aged;\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n452\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n453\t//     from SSE error events\n454\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n455\t\n456\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n457\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n458\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n459\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n460\t    router_unavailable: {\n461\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n462\t        icon: '🔌',\n463\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n464\t    },\n465\t    all_sources_failed: {\n466\t        title: 'No data available',\n467\t        icon: '📭',\n468\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n469\t    },\n470\t    synthesis_failed: {\n471\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n472\t        icon: '⚠️',\n473\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n474\t    },\n475\t    malformed_router_output: {\n476\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n477\t        icon: '🧩',\n478\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n479\t    },\n480\t    no_match: {\n481\t        title: 'No matching project',\n482\t        icon: '❓',\n483\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n484\t    }\n485\t};\n486\t\n487\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n488\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n489\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n490\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t/**\n494\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n495\t *\n496\t * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n497\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n498\t *\n499\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n500\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n501\t */\n502\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n503\t    data = data || {};\n504\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n505\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n506\t\n507\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n508\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n509\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n510\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n511\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n512\t\n513\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n514\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n515\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n516\t    ]));\n517\t\n518\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n519\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n520\t\n521\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n522\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n523\t    if (data.utterance) {\n524\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n525\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n526\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n527\t        ]));\n528\t    }\n529\t\n530\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n531\t    const sources = data.sources;\n532\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n533\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n534\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n535\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n536\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n537\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n538\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n539\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n540\t            ]));\n541\t        });\n542\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n543\t    }\n544\t\n545\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n546\t    if (data.data != null) {\n547\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n548\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n549\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n550\t    }\n551\t\n552\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n553\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n554\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n555\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n556\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n557\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n558\t        });\n559\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n560\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n561\t            ul\n562\t        ]));\n563\t    }\n564\t\n565\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n566\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n567\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n568\t        : 'Retry';\n569\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n570\t\n571\t    return card;\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n575\t// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n576\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n577\t\n578\t/**\n579\t * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n580\t * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that is stuck\n581\t * @property {string} stuck_reason - The reason why the bead is stuck (refusal reason)\n582\t * @property {number} [refusal_count] - Number of refusals that caused the stuck state\n583\t * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the stuck state\n584\t * @property {string} [action_hint] - Optional hint for user action to resolve\n585\t */\n586\t\n587\t/**\n588\t * @typedef {Object} FailedCardData\n589\t * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that failed\n590\t * @property {string} failure_reason - The reason why the task failed\n591\t * @property {string} [error_type] - Optional error type/category\n592\t * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the failure\n593\t */\n594\t\n595\t/**\n596\t * @typedef {Object} ErrorCardData\n597\t * @property {string} error_type - The error variant type\n598\t * @property {string} [utterance] - The original user utterance\n599\t * @property {string} [detail] - Error detail message\n600\t * @property {Array<{name?: string, source?: string, reason?: string, error?: string}>} [sources] - Failed sources list\n601\t * @property {*} [data] - Raw degraded data\n602\t * @property {string} [intent_id] - Associated intent ID\n603\t * @property {string} [pending_id] - Associated pending ID\n604\t * @property {Array<string|{slug?: string, name?: string}>} [registered_projects] - Projects for no-match variant\n605\t */\n606\t\n607\t/**\n608\t * @typedef {Object} PendingCardData\n609\t * @property {string} utterance - The utterance being processed\n610\t * @property {number} createdAt - Creation timestamp (ms epoch)\n611\t * @property {string} [pendingId] - Stable ID for this placeholder\n612\t * @property {number} [completed] - Number of completed sources (for progress)\n613\t * @property {number} [total] - Total number of sources (for progress)\n614\t */\n615\t\n616\t/**\n617\t * @typedef {Object} TopicCardData\n618\t * @property {Object} topic - The topic object\n619\t * @property {string} topic.id - Topic ID\n620\t * @property {string} topic.label - Topic label\n621\t * @property {string} [topic.type] - Topic type (project, research, personal, exception, compound)\n622\t * @property {Object} staleness - Staleness info\n623\t * @property {number} staleness.seconds - Seconds since last update\n624\t * @property {Object} [latest_result] - Optional latest result\n625\t * @property {string} latest_result.summary - Result summary\n626\t * @property {string} [latest_result.urgency] - Result urgency (critical, high, normal, low)\n627\t * @property {*} [latest_result.data] - Result data\n628\t */\n629\t\n630\t/**\n631\t * @typedef {Object} FallbackCardData\n632\t * @property {string} [summary] - Result summary\n633\t * @property {*} [data] - Result data\n634\t * @property {string} [urgency] - Result urgency\n635\t */\n636\t\n637\t/**\n638\t * @typedef {StuckCardData | FailedCardData | ErrorCardData | PendingCardData | TopicCardData | FallbackCardData} CardData\n639\t * Union type for all card data structures\n640\t */\n641\t\n642\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n643\t// (5) Stuck/Failed cards — circuit breaker and terminal failure cards\n644\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n645\t\n646\t/**\n647\t * Build a stuck card for a fenced bead.\n648\t *\n649\t * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when a bead is fenced due to repeated\n650\t * refusals or timeout. Shows the bead reference, refusal count, and latest reason.\n651\t *\n652\t * @param {StuckCardData} data - Stuck card data structure\n653\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .stuck-card element (data-builtin=\"stuck\")\n654\t */\n655\tfunction createStuckCard(data) {\n656\t    data = data || {};\n657\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card stuck-card');\n658\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'stuck';\n659\t    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n660\t\n661\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n662\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🚧']),\n663\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task stuck — needs your input'])\n664\t    ]));\n665\t\n666\t    if (data.message) {\n667\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-message', [data.message]));\n668\t    }\n669\t\n670\t    if (data.stuck_reason) {\n671\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'stuck-reason-wrap', [\n672\t            el('span', 'stuck-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n673\t            el('span', 'stuck-reason', [data.stuck_reason])\n674\t        ]));\n675\t    }\n676\t\n677\t    const meta = el('div', 'stuck-meta');\n678\t    if (data.refusal_count != null) {\n679\t        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-refusal-count', [\n680\t            'Refusals: ' + String(data.refusal_count)\n681\t        ]));\n682\t    }\n683\t    if (data.bead_id) {\n684\t        if (meta.children.length > 0) {\n685\t            meta.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' • '));\n686\t        }\n687\t        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n688\t    }\n689\t    if (meta.children.length > 0) {\n690\t        card.appendChild(meta);\n691\t    }\n692\t\n693\t    if (data.action_hint) {\n694\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-action-hint', [data.action_hint]));\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    // View bead button\n698\t    const viewBtn = el('button', 'stuck-view-bead', ['View bead']);\n699\t    card.appendChild(viewBtn);\n700\t\n701\t    return card;\n702\t}\n703\t\n704\t/**\n705\t * Build a failed card for a terminal failure.\n706\t *\n707\t * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when an intent fails non-recoverably\n708\t * (worker crash, invalid input, etc.). Shows failure reason and context.\n709\t *\n710\t * @param {FailedCardData} data - Failed card data structure\n711\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .failed-card element (data-builtin=\"failed\")\n712\t */\n713\tfunction createFailedCard(data) {\n714\t    data = data || {};\n715\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card failed-card');\n716\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'failed';\n717\t    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n718\t\n719\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n720\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['❌']),\n721\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task failed'])\n722\t    ]));\n723\t\n724\t    if (data.message) {\n725\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'failed-message', [data.message]));\n726\t    }\n727\t\n728\t    if (data.failure_reason) {\n729\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-reason-wrap', [\n730\t            el('span', 'failed-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n731\t            el('span', 'failed-reason', [data.failure_reason])\n732\t        ]));\n733\t    }\n734\t\n735\t    if (data.error_type) {\n736\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-error-type', [\n737\t            'Error type: ' + data.error_type\n738\t        ]));\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    if (data.bead_id) {\n742\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n743\t    }\n744\t\n745\t    // Retry button\n746\t    const retryBtn = el('button', 'failed-retry', ['Retry']);\n747\t    card.appendChild(retryBtn);\n748\t\n749\t    return card;\n750\t}\n751\t\n752\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n753\t// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n754\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n755\t\n756\t/**\n757\t * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n758\t * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n759\t * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n760\t * rendering escaping contract.\n761\t *\n762\t * @param {FallbackCardData} result - Fallback card data structure\n763\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n764\t */\n765\tfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n766\t    result = result || {};\n767\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n768\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n769\t\n770\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n771\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n772\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n773\t    ]));\n774\t\n775\t    if (result.summary) {\n776\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n777\t    }\n778\t\n779\t    const data = result.data;\n780\t    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n781\t    if (rows.length) {\n782\t        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n783\t        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n784\t            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n785\t            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n786\t        });\n787\t        card.appendChild(grid);\n788\t    }\n789\t\n790\t    if (result.urgency) {\n791\t        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n792\t    }\n793\t\n794\t    return card;\n795\t}\n796\t\n797\t// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\n798\tfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n799\t    if (data == null) return [];\n800\t    let obj = data;\n801\t    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n802\t        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n803\t    }\n804\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n805\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n806\t    }\n807\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n808\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n809\t    }\n810\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n814\t    if (v == null) return '';\n815\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n816\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n817\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n818\t}\n819\t\n820\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n821\t// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\n822\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n823\t    module.exports = {\n824\t        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n825\t        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n826\t        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n827\t        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n828\t        // Built-in card families + helpers\n829\t        el: el,\n830\t        formatElapsed: formatElapsed,\n831\t        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n832\t        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n833\t        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n834\t        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n835\t        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n836\t        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n837\t        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n838\t        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n839\t        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n840\t        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n841\t        createStuckCard: createStuckCard,\n842\t        createFailedCard: createFailedCard,\n843\t        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n844\t        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n845\t        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n846\t        _setProgress: _setProgress,  // Also export internal helper\n847\t        _normalizeVariant: _normalizeVariant,  // Also export for testing\n848\t        // Type definitions (for documentation and IDE autocomplete)\n849\t        types: {\n850\t            StuckCardData: 'StuckCardData',\n851\t            FailedCardData: 'FailedCardData',\n852\t            ErrorCardData: 'ErrorCardData',\n853\t            PendingCardData: 'PendingCardData',\n854\t            TopicCardData: 'TopicCardData',\n855\t            FallbackCardData: 'FallbackCardData',\n856\t            CardData: 'CardData'  // Union type\n857\t        }\n858\t    };\n859\t}\n860\t\n861\t// Also expose to inline script in browser (buildContainerChildren is used by loadTopics)\n862\tif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n863\t    window.buildContainerChildren = buildContainerChildren;\n864\t    window._setProgress = _setProgress;\n865\t    window.el = el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n866\t    window.createErrorCard = createErrorCard;  // Error card renderer for SSE error events\n867\t    window.createStuckCard = createStuckCard;  // Stuck card renderer for task_stuck events\n868\t    window.createFailedCard = createFailedCard;  // Failed card renderer for task_failed events\n869\t    // Expose type definitions for IDE autocomplete and documentation\n870\t    window.CanvasCardTypes = {\n871\t        StuckCardData: 'StuckCardData',\n872\t        FailedCardData: 'FailedCardData',\n873\t        ErrorCardData: 'ErrorCardData',\n874\t        PendingCardData: 'PendingCardData',\n875\t        TopicCardData: 'TopicCardData',\n876\t        FallbackCardData: 'FallbackCardData',\n877\t        CardData: 'CardData'  // Union type\n878\t    };\n879\t}\n880\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"97d79bb9-501c-40d0-86e4-0e85ce71cd4d","uuid":"11ab75e2-ec06-4194-a3c6-fae45b9be72a","timestamp":"2026-07-23T08:51:22.544Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/canvas/canvas.js","content":"/*\n * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n    var document = global.document;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a topic card from topic data.\n *\n * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element\n */\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n *\n * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n * inserted verbatim as text.\n */\nfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n    if (className) node.className = className;\n    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n        if (c == null) return;\n        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n        } else {\n            node.appendChild(c);\n        }\n    });\n    return node;\n}\n\n// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\nfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n    const rem = s % 60;\n    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\nvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n];\n\n// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n// projects' supported intents\").\nvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n};\n\nfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n    const out = [];\n    const seen = {};\n    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n        return ad - bd;\n    });\n    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n            if (!tmpl) return;\n            seen[intent] = true;\n            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n        });\n    });\n    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n}\n\n// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\nvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n\n/**\n * Build the first-run welcome card.\n *\n * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n *\n * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n */\nfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n        const desc = p.description || '';\n        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n        list.appendChild(li);\n    });\n    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n    }\n    card.appendChild(list);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n    });\n    card.appendChild(examples);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n *\n * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n *\n * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n *\n * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n */\nfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n    }\n    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n *\n * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n    ]));\n\n    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n\n    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n    if (data.utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n    const sources = data.sources;\n    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n            ]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(ul);\n    }\n\n    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n    if (data.data != null) {\n        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n    }\n\n    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n            ul\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n        ? 'Edit & resend'\n        : 'Retry';\n    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that is stuck\n * @property {string} stuck_reason - The reason why the bead is stuck (refusal reason)\n * @property {number} [refusal_count] - Number of refusals that caused the stuck state\n * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the stuck state\n * @property {string} [action_hint] - Optional hint for user action to resolve\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} FailedCardData\n * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that failed\n * @property {string} failure_reason - The reason why the task failed\n * @property {string} [error_type] - Optional error type/category\n * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the failure\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} ErrorCardData\n * @property {string} error_type - The error variant type\n * @property {string} [utterance] - The original user utterance\n * @property {string} [detail] - Error detail message\n * @property {Array<{name?: string, source?: string, reason?: string, error?: string}>} [sources] - Failed sources list\n * @property {*} [data] - Raw degraded data\n * @property {string} [intent_id] - Associated intent ID\n * @property {string} [pending_id] - Associated pending ID\n * @property {Array<string|{slug?: string, name?: string}>} [registered_projects] - Projects for no-match variant\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} PendingCardData\n * @property {string} utterance - The utterance being processed\n * @property {number} createdAt - Creation timestamp (ms epoch)\n * @property {string} [pendingId] - Stable ID for this placeholder\n * @property {number} [completed] - Number of completed sources (for progress)\n * @property {number} [total] - Total number of sources (for progress)\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} TopicCardData\n * @property {Object} topic - The topic object\n * @property {string} topic.id - Topic ID\n * @property {string} topic.label - Topic label\n * @property {string} [topic.type] - Topic type (project, research, personal, exception, compound)\n * @property {Object} staleness - Staleness info\n * @property {number} staleness.seconds - Seconds since last update\n * @property {Object} [latest_result] - Optional latest result\n * @property {string} latest_result.summary - Result summary\n * @property {string} [latest_result.urgency] - Result urgency (critical, high, normal, low)\n * @property {*} [latest_result.data] - Result data\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} FallbackCardData\n * @property {string} [summary] - Result summary\n * @property {*} [data] - Result data\n * @property {string} [urgency] - Result urgency\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {StuckCardData | FailedCardData | ErrorCardData | PendingCardData | TopicCardData | FallbackCardData} CardData\n * Union type for all card data structures\n */\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (5) Stuck/Failed cards — circuit breaker and terminal failure cards\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a stuck card for a fenced bead.\n *\n * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when a bead is fenced due to repeated\n * refusals or timeout. Shows the bead reference, refusal count, and latest reason.\n *\n * @param {StuckCardData} data - Stuck card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .stuck-card element (data-builtin=\"stuck\")\n */\nfunction createStuckCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card stuck-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'stuck';\n    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🚧']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task stuck — needs your input'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (data.message) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-message', [data.message]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.stuck_reason) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'stuck-reason-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'stuck-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n            el('span', 'stuck-reason', [data.stuck_reason])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    const meta = el('div', 'stuck-meta');\n    if (data.refusal_count != null) {\n        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-refusal-count', [\n            'Refusals: ' + String(data.refusal_count)\n        ]));\n    }\n    if (data.bead_id) {\n        if (meta.children.length > 0) {\n            meta.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' • '));\n        }\n        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n    }\n    if (meta.children.length > 0) {\n        card.appendChild(meta);\n    }\n\n    if (data.action_hint) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-action-hint', [data.action_hint]));\n    }\n\n    // View bead button\n    const viewBtn = el('button', 'stuck-view-bead', ['View bead']);\n    card.appendChild(viewBtn);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a failed card for a terminal failure.\n *\n * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when an intent fails non-recoverably\n * (worker crash, invalid input, etc.). Shows failure reason and context.\n *\n * @param {FailedCardData} data - Failed card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .failed-card element (data-builtin=\"failed\")\n */\nfunction createFailedCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card failed-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'failed';\n    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['❌']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task failed'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (data.message) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'failed-message', [data.message]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.failure_reason) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-reason-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'failed-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n            el('span', 'failed-reason', [data.failure_reason])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.error_type) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-error-type', [\n            'Error type: ' + data.error_type\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.bead_id) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n    }\n\n    // Retry button\n    const retryBtn = el('button', 'failed-retry', ['Retry']);\n    card.appendChild(retryBtn);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n * rendering escaping contract.\n *\n * @param {FallbackCardData} result - Fallback card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n */\nfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n    result = result || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (result.summary) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n    }\n\n    const data = result.data;\n    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n    if (rows.length) {\n        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(grid);\n    }\n\n    if (result.urgency) {\n        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n    }\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\nfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n    if (data == null) return [];\n    let obj = data;\n    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n    }\n    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n    }\n    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n}\n\nfunction _stringify(v) {\n    if (v == null) return '';\n    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations).
